name: Mindelo
summary: Mindelo is a colourful port town on the volcanic island of São Vicente, off the west coast of Africa. It sits around a huge blue bay, watched over by Monte Cara, a mountain shaped like a giant sleeping face.
funFacts: Across the bay stands Monte Cara, a 490-metre volcanic peak whose ridges look exactly like a human face gazing up at the sky.
Mindelo's most famous singer, Cesaria Evora, was called the Barefoot Diva because she always sang on stage with no shoes on.
Every February the town throws Cape Verde's biggest Carnival, with feathered costumes, drumming bands and a special children's parade through the streets.
Cape Verde is ten volcanic islands that popped up out of the Atlantic Ocean, and nobody lived on Sao Vicente at all until the 1790s.
Loggerhead turtles swim hundreds of kilometres to lay their eggs on Cape Verde's beaches — one of the biggest turtle nesting spots in the world.
thingsToDo: Look out across Porto Grande Bay to spot the sleeping-giant face shape on Monte Cara mountain.
Wander Praca Nova, the leafy main square where local families meet under tall trees and pastel buildings.
Dig your toes into the soft golden sand at Praia da Laginha, a calm city beach near the cruise dock.
Visit the colourful Mercado Municipal to spot tropical fruit, fresh fish and chatty vendors selling spices.
Find the blue house of Cesaria Evora, the Barefoot Diva, now kept as a little museum about her life.
Walk down Rua de Lisboa to see brightly painted Portuguese-era buildings in pink, yellow and blue.

wildlife.name: Loggerhead Sea Turtle
wildlife.description: Big-headed sea turtle that crawls onto Cape Verde's beaches each summer to lay its eggs.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean animal that swims past Cape Verde's islands to have its babies in warm water.

wildlife.name: Magnificent Frigatebird
wildlife.description: Big black seabird with long pointy wings that glides for hours over the bay.

wildlife.name: Sergeant Major Fish
wildlife.description: Small reef fish with bright yellow and black stripes that zips around the rocks.

localWord.meaning: A friendly Kriolu greeting meaning 'Everything all right?' that people say all day long.

images.alt: View across Porto Grande Bay in Mindelo with Monte Cara mountain rising on the far shore.
Monte Cara, the volcanic mountain shaped like a sleeping face, across the bay from Mindelo.
Praca Nova, the leafy main square in the heart of Mindelo where families gather under the trees.
A bright, colourful old colonial building on a Mindelo street showing the town's Portuguese-era architecture.
Praia da Laginha, Mindelo's golden city beach with calm turquoise water perfect for a swim.
The blue-painted house of famous Cape Verdean singer Cesaria Evora, a much-loved Mindelo landmark.
A dancer in a bright costume during the famous Mindelo Carnival parade.
A loggerhead sea turtle swimming in clear blue water.
A humpback whale leaping out of the ocean.
A magnificent frigatebird gliding with its long pointed wings spread wide.
A sergeant major fish with bright yellow and black stripes swimming near a reef.
